hyperedges.label: Phase A Sessions (A0-A11) Forming the Path to Gate GA
Five Core Modules Surviving Phase A (C1-C5)
Track 2 Hardening Sessions (B5-B10) Running Parallel to Forward Test

nodes.label: Jobs Rule (Scope Deletion)
nodes.rationale: Phase A builds only what answers whether the liquidity sweep edge survives walk-forward validation; everything else is deferred regardless of how cheap it appears, translating Jobs' ~70% product-line deletion into module/table/state cuts.

nodes.label: Musk Rule (Sequencing)
nodes.rationale: Strict order: interrogate requirements to make them less dumb, delete, simplify/optimize only after deleting, accelerate cycle time, automate last. Everything parallelizable runs in parallel; if you don't add back ~10% later, you didn't delete enough.

nodes.label: Physics Rule (Launch Windows)
nodes.rationale: The 8-week forward test and trade-count-gated outcome learning are non-negotiable physics (calendar time and statistical sample size) -- no early exit on good results, no extension-as-rescue on bad ones, no mid-window parameter changes. All schedule aggression must land on build scope and sequencing, never on these gates.

nodes.label: Determinism Contract (Golden-Fixture Replay)
nodes.rationale: Enforced in CI from session A6 onward: a fixed sample month replays twice per commit and both runs must produce identical output hashes (signals, trades, equity). Any nondeterminism -- dict ordering, float accumulation order, output timestamps -- is a build failure.

nodes.label: Config Hash / config_version System
nodes.rationale: Every config is a YAML file per instrument loaded into a frozen dataclass, canonicalised, sha256-hashed to config_hash, and inserted-or-fetched in config_version. Identical configs must always produce identical hashes so walk-forward results are traceable and reproducible -- without this, walk-forward results are meaningless.

nodes.label: Spec Immutability During Phase A
nodes.rationale: The v2.1 spec is immutable during Phase A; any proposed strategy change is written to docs/decisions/ and parked until after the GO/NO-GO verdict, because changing the strategy while validating it invalidates the validation.

nodes.label: Phase A: Prove (Weeks 1-3)
nodes.rationale: Single deliverable is the Verdict Report, with Gate GA (GO/NO-GO) at end of week 3. A backtest in Phase A is a pure function with zero operating states -- compressed from v2.1's week-7 gate by deleting non-essential platform scaffolding while keeping thresholds and multi-instrument requirements unchanged.

nodes.label: Phase B: Forward Test + Parallel Hardening (Weeks 4-13)
nodes.rationale: Track 1 runs the frozen 8-week forward test (the launch window); Track 2 builds hardening (live tables, risk modules, monitoring, Docker, VPS shadow, state-machine interrogation, Graphify) in parallel, deployed only after Gate GB, so the calendar's idle engineering time is eliminated without compressing validation.
nodes.norm_label: phase b: forward test + parallel hardening (weeks 4-13)

nodes.label: Phase C: Scale (Week 13+)
nodes.rationale: Promotes the burned-in Track 2 build to VPS, applies Musk's 10% rule by reviewing the deleted-items list and adding back only what is genuinely missed (with a written ADR per item), and arms outcome learning behind its trade-count gate.

nodes.label: Phase A Schema (migration 001_core.sql)
nodes.rationale: Reduced from v2.1's seven-table DDL to four tables for Phase A because live-trading tables serve no purpose pre-GO; the three live-trading tables are restored verbatim in Phase B Track 2 since their DDL already exists and deferral costs nothing.

nodes.label: Session A0: Scaffold
nodes.rationale: Creates the reduced repo structure (src/lsb/{data,signals,backtest,walkforward,report}/, config/, migrations/, tests/, docs/decisions/, scripts/), writes PHASE_STATUS.md locking Phases B/C, carries forward ADR-001, sets up pytest+CI. Deliberately omits broker, state-machine, Docker, deployment dirs because they do not exist in Phase A.

nodes.label: Sessions A4-A5: Signal Engine (Eight Gates)
nodes.rationale: Gates 1-4 (A4) and 5-8 (A5) implemented as pure functions over candle windows, test-driven against hand-labeled fixtures with near-miss rejections; a setup qualifies only when all 8 gates pass on the same evaluation, plus a shuffle property test ensuring shuffled candle history never qualifies at a rate comparable to real history.

nodes.label: Sessions A6-A7: Backtest Engine + SimulatedBroker
nodes.rationale: A6 builds the event-driven backtest core with an injectable Clock and position lifecycle (pending->filled->managed->closed); A7 implements SimulatedBroker with the v2.1 Part 7.1 pessimistic fill model (spread costs, ATR-scaled slippage, gap-through-stop fills, commission/swap), unit-tested against hand-computed expected fills so no fill is ever better than the pessimistic model allows.

nodes.label: Session A9: Walk-Forward Harness
nodes.rationale: Rolling 18-month train / 6-month test windows orchestrated across all loaded instruments, each window running the deterministic engine and persisting one wf_run row plus child sim_trade rows keyed to config_hash; windows are independent and run in parallel processes, with no window's test span overlapping its own train span.

nodes.label: Session A10: Verdict Report
nodes.rationale: Per-window and aggregate metrics, equity curves, drawdown profiles, per-window stability analysis, and a machine-rendered GO/NO-GO verdict evaluated strictly against Blueprint v2.1 Part 14 thresholds read from spec config (never hard-coded or re-typed), output to docs/verdict_report.md with rendered charts.

nodes.label: Session A11: Full Run + Gate GA (GO/NO-GO)
nodes.rationale: Executes the complete walk-forward across all instruments on audited data, reviews anomalies in docs/anomaly_review.md, renders the Verdict Report, and records the verdict in ADR-002-go-no-go.md. On NO-GO: stop, no Phase B work begins, archive the run, write a post-mortem, and either treat parked docs/decisions/ ideas as hypotheses for a new ~3-week Phase A cycle or retire the strategy -- a NO-GO at week 3 costing zero infrastructure is the plan working as designed.
nodes.norm_label: session a11: full run + gate ga (go/no-go)

nodes.label: Session B1: MT5 Demo Bridge
nodes.rationale: Implements the broker bridge per ADR-001 (native-Windows MT5, EA-socket fallback) conforming to the same broker interface SimulatedBroker implements, verified by a conformance test suite both brokers pass identically. Demo account only.

nodes.label: Session B2: Five-State Operating Machine
nodes.rationale: IDLE -> SCANNING -> PENDING_ENTRY -> IN_TRADE -> HALTED, each transition writing a state_transition row with reason. HALTED is reachable from every state on data integrity failure, broker disconnect, daily-loss limit breach, or manual command, and requires manual acknowledgment to exit.

nodes.label: Session B3: Live Loop Wiring
nodes.rationale: Wires the unchanged signal engine and trade management into the live evaluation loop with Clock in live mode and the MT5 demo bridge, using the same signal schema as backtest so forward-test data is directly comparable to walk-forward data; adds a daily integrity audit job that transitions to HALTED on failure.

nodes.label: Session B4: Forward Test Launch
nodes.rationale: Launch checklist requires config_hash frozen and recorded, Gate GB thresholds written down before the test starts (pre-registration), and 48h of green daily audits -- this pre-registration prevents the bar from drifting after results are seen.

nodes.label: The Freeze Rule (Weeks 5-13)
nodes.rationale: No parameter changes, no gate-logic changes, no small fixes to strategy code for the 8-week forward test. Infrastructure defect fixes (logging, reconnects) are allowed with a written entry in docs/forward_test_log.md; anything touching signal or trade-management logic restarts the 8-week window. Weekly review is observation only -- no early termination on good results, no extension-as-rescue on bad ones.

nodes.label: Track 2: Hardening (Sessions B5-B10)
nodes.rationale: Built alongside the running forward test and deployed only after Gate GB so it never touches the frozen forward-test process: restores remaining v2.1 tables (002_live.sql), implements risk/portfolio M-modules, monitoring/alerting, Docker images, a VPS shadow deployment for burn-in, state-machine interrogation, and Graphify seeding.

nodes.label: Session B5: Restore Remaining v2.1 Tables (002_live.sql)
nodes.norm_label: session b5: restore remaining v2.1 tables (002_live.sql)

nodes.label: Session B6: Risk and Portfolio Modules
nodes.rationale: Implements the deferred M-modules (position sizing, exposure caps, daily-loss enforcement) test-driven against v2.1 risk parameters, feeding the HALTED trigger.
nodes.norm_label: session b6: risk and portfolio modules

nodes.label: Session B7: Monitoring and Alerting
nodes.rationale: Heartbeats, state-transition alerts, daily digest -- designed to alert on silence, not just on errors.

nodes.label: Session B9: VPS Provisioning + Shadow Deployment
nodes.rationale: VPS provisioned and security-hardened but not promoted; the forward test stays in place while the VPS runs a shadow deployment against demo to burn in.
nodes.norm_label: session b9: vps provisioning + shadow deployment

nodes.label: Session B10: Two Interrogations (Deleted States + Graphify Seeding)
nodes.rationale: (a) Each of the four deleted v2.1 operating states returns only with a written concrete failure scenario it uniquely handles, recorded as an ADR, else it stays deleted. (b) Graphify seeding: pip install graphifyy (double y), graphify install --platform claude, /graphify . at repo root with /docs holding the v2.1 blueprint, the v3.0 spec, and this plan; --postgres introspection once the full schema exists; commit graphify-out/. Ongoing: graphify update . after merges, graphify affected before any risk-math change.
nodes.norm_label: session b10: two interrogations (deleted states + graphify seeding)

nodes.label: Gate GA: GO/NO-GO (End of Week 3)
nodes.rationale: Criteria are Blueprint v2.1 Part 14, unchanged: multi-instrument requirement, per-window stability tests, aggregate thresholds. The gate moved from week 7 to week 3 but the bar did not move.

nodes.label: Gate GA-1: Determinism in CI
nodes.rationale: Two consecutive CI runs of the golden-fixture replay must produce identical hashes, and an intentionally injected nondeterminism must be caught by the gate.

nodes.label: Gate GB: Forward-Test Verdict (Week 13)
nodes.rationale: Compares the forward-test trade distribution against walk-forward expectations (distribution comparison, not cherry-picked headline metrics). Trade-count sufficiency applies: if 8 weeks produce fewer trades than the v2.1 minimum, the verdict is INSUFFICIENT and the test extends in whole-week increments until the count gate is met -- trade count is physics, the calendar serves it. On NO-GO: VPS is not promoted, no live account opens, and divergence analysis feeds a new Phase A cycle while Track 2 work is retained as strategy-agnostic.

nodes.label: Session C1: Promotion
nodes.rationale: Cutover checklist: VPS shadow deployment has >=2 weeks clean burn-in, monitoring alert paths verified end-to-end, HALTED->manual-acknowledge flow drilled once deliberately. Promote to VPS on demo for one final week, then only on explicit manual decision (never automated) go minimum-size live per the v2.1 capital schedule.

nodes.label: Session C2: The 10% Review
nodes.rationale: Walks the full Section 1.4/section-2 delete list from the v3.0 spec; each candidate returns only with a written justification ADR. If nothing returns, the deletions were correct; if more than ~10% returns, the Phase A cuts were too shallow -- recorded for the next project.

nodes.label: Session C3: Outcome Learning Activation
nodes.rationale: The learning loop activates only when live trade count crosses the v2.1 minimum (trade-count-gated exactly per v2.1), and updates are batched per the spec, never per-trade. Hiring triggers remain tied to v2.1 revenue milestones.

nodes.label: ADR-001: Broker Bridge Decision
nodes.rationale: Decides native-Windows MT5 with EA-socket fallback, status accepted, implementation deferred to Session B1.

nodes.label: Pessimistic Fill Model (Blueprint v2.1 Part 7.1)
nodes.rationale: Spread costs, ATR-scaled slippage, gap-through-stop fills at the gapped price, commission and swap -- ensures no simulated fill is ever better than this pessimistic model allows, inherited unchanged from v2.1.

nodes.label: Eight Entry Gates (Strategy Logic)
nodes.rationale: Untouchable -- this is the strategy under test, not scope to be cut. A setup qualifies only when all eight gates pass on the same evaluation, per v2.1.

nodes.label: GO/NO-GO Thresholds (Blueprint v2.1 Part 14)
nodes.rationale: Bit-identical thresholds used by both Gate GA and Gate GB verdict logic; both gates' criteria live in spec config read by the verdict code so softening the bar requires a visible diff to a protected file, reviewable as an ADR.

nodes.label: Risk Register (v2.1 + v3.0 Deltas)
nodes.rationale: v2.1 register carries forward in full with three updates: schedule pressure eroding validation gates (top risk, strengthened mitigation via spec-config-driven verdict criteria), parallel-track contamination (new -- forward test on isolated tagged-release infrastructure, Track 2 develops on main), and compression spillover (new -- mitigated by the freeze rule and pre-registered GB thresholds).

nodes.label: Module Cut: M1-M14 to Five Core Modules
nodes.rationale: v2.1's 14 modules (M1-M14) are cut to 5 core modules (C1 Data Pipeline, C2 Signal Engine, C3 Backtest Engine, C4 Walk-Forward Harness, C5 Verdict Report) for Phase A because only these answer the edge question; the rest are deferred not destroyed, rebuilt in Phase B's parallel track or interrogated again before being added back.
nodes.norm_label: module cut: m1-m14 to five core modules

nodes.label: C1: Data Pipeline
nodes.rationale: Historical candle ingestion, integrity checks, gap handling; absorbs the v2.1 data modules.

nodes.label: C2: Signal Engine
nodes.rationale: The 8 entry gates, exactly per v2.1 spec -- pure functions, no broker awareness.

nodes.label: C3: Backtest Engine
nodes.rationale: Deterministic, event-driven, pessimistic fill simulation per v2.1 Part 7.1 (spread, ATR-scaled slippage, gap-through-stop fills, commission/swap).

nodes.label: C4: Walk-Forward Harness
nodes.rationale: Rolling 18-month train / 6-month test windows, 3 instruments, automated window orchestration.

nodes.label: C5: Verdict Report
nodes.rationale: Metrics, equity curves, per-window stability, machine-rendered GO/NO-GO verdict against v2.1 Part 14 thresholds.

nodes.label: State Machine Cut: 9 -> 0 (Phase A) -> 5 (Phase B)
nodes.rationale: A backtest has no broker, no orders, no halts, so Phase A needs zero operating states; Phase B needs five (IDLE/SCANNING/PENDING_ENTRY/IN_TRADE/HALTED); the remaining four v2.1 states must each re-justify their existence with a concrete failure scenario, recorded as an ADR, before Phase C.

nodes.label: Schema Cut: 7 Tables -> 4 (Phase A)
nodes.rationale: Live-trading tables serve no purpose pre-GO, so Phase A keeps only config_version, candle, signal, wf_run (+ sim_trade); the three live-trading tables are restored verbatim in Phase B since their DDL already exists and deferral costs nothing.

hyperedges.label: Phase A Sessions (A0-A11) Forming the Path to Gate GA
Five Core Modules Surviving Phase A (C1-C5)
Track 2 Hardening Sessions (B5-B10) Running Parallel to Forward Test
